title: 6 Cash Services User Management
body_text:
6	Cash Services User Management
6.1	Overview of Cash Services User Management
There are two user management components for Cash Services Users. First, users must be set up as Client Centre Users or CPS Admin Security Officers with access to Cash Services.
See the User Management Part or the CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on creating or modifying Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers and providing access to Cash Services.
Note:	Currently, there are two user management applications running parallel: CPS Admin and User Management. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin. CPS Admin Security Officers are encouraged to use the User Management application.
Second, Admin Users and Access Management Users create profiles for other users in Cash Services. Initially, Royal Bank sets up two Admin or Access Management Users, who can then set up other users as required by your financial institution.
Authorized users access Cash Services through Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Client Centre. See Section 5.2, Accessing Cash Services via the Client Centre.
6.2	Cash Services User Types
In Cash Services, Admin Users and Access Management Users can create profiles for the following types of users:
Regular Users (e-Client)
ГўВҖВ“ Can enter and manage cash orders. This includes editing an existing order up to its cut-off time and approving an order if this option is enabled for your financial institution. Regular Users canГўВҖВҷt manage user profiles.
Admin Users (e-Client Admin)
ГўВҖВ“ Can set up and maintain profiles for all user types. This includes creating new users, amending or suspending existing users, resetting passwords, and unlocking user accounts. Admin Users can also enter and manage cash orders.
Access Management Users (e-Client Access Mgmt)
ГўВҖВ“ Can set up and maintain profiles for all user types. This includes creating new users, amending or suspending existing users, resetting passwords, and unlocking user accounts. Access Management Users canГўВҖВҷt enter or manage cash orders.
6.3	Creating User Profiles in Cash Services
Individuals must be set up as Client Centre Users or Security Officers with access to Cash Services before their profile can be set up in Cash Services. Admin Users and Access Management Users can then create profiles in Cash Services for all user types.
To create a profile in Cash Services:
Log in to Cash Services as described in Section 5.2, Accessing Cash Services via the Client Centre.
Select
Configuration
>
Profiles
>
User
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Configuring a User Profile
Select
Add User
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Selecting Add User
Enter the individualГўВҖВҷs email address and name, then select the language and status. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Entering User Credentials
Scroll down the User Profile page to complete the other sections.
Enter the one-time password, then select the Temporary Password box to prompt the user to change the password at the initial login. See Figure 4. Users must update temporary passwords within 48 hours.
Select the User Role from the Role drop-down menu. See Figure 4. See Section 6.2, Cash Services User Types, for descriptions of the available user types for financial institutions.
Important
:	Only the three ГўВҖВңeClientГўВҖВқ roles are configured for financial institutions. The ГўВҖВңBranchГўВҖВқ role types are for RBCГўВҖВҷs internal use.
Figure 4: Entering one-time Password and Selecting User Role
Assign the user with access to locations as described below. You can only assign users access to locations that you have access to.
Note:	The External ID Number field isnГўВҖВҷt used for Cash Services user setup, and the Upload Image feature doesnГўВҖВҷt apply.
To select one location for the user:
ГўВ‘
Enter the location number in the Locations Filter field and select search (magnifying glass). See Figure 5.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the box beside the location.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select
Add >>
to assign the location.
Figure 5: Filtering to Select One Location
To select multiple locations for the user:
ГўВ‘
Select the SELECT ALL box or select the individual locations you want to give the user access to. See Figure 6.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select
Add >>
to assign the locations.
Figure 6: Selecting and Assigning Multiple Locations
Select
Create User
to complete the profile. See Figure 5 and Figure 6 above.
The system creates a Login ID for the individual and the profile is added to the list of users on the User Profile page. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: New User Profile Completed
6.4	Modifying Cash Services User Profiles
Admin Users and Access Management Users can modify profiles for all other Cash Services user types (e.g., email address, passwords, status, role, locations).
To modify a userГўВҖВҷs profile:
Log in to Cash Services as described in Section 5.2, Accessing Cash Services via the Client Centre.
Select
Configuration
>
Profiles
>
User
. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Logging in to Modify User Profiles
To locate the user profile you want to update:
ГўВ‘
Enter search criteria in the Filter section to locate the user. See Figure 9.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Make sure the Status selected is
All
.
If the status isnГўВҖВҷt set to All, users with a locked profile wonГўВҖВҷt show in the search results.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select the Edit icon in the Actions column to open the profile page.
Figure 9: Searching for the User and Opening the Profile
Update the userГўВҖВҷs profile, as applicable, then select
Update User
to complete the change. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Saving User Profile Changes
6.5	Resetting a UserГўВҖВҷs Password
Admin Users and Access Management Users can reset passwords for all other Cash Services Users.
Note:	If a user makes three invalid login attempts, the user is locked out and the account becomes inactive. See Section 6.7, Unlocking a UserГўВҖВҷs Account.
To reset a password:
Log in to Cash Services as described in Section 5.2, Accessing Cash Services via the Client Centre.
Select
Configuration
>
Profiles
>
User
. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Logging in to Modify User Profiles
Locate the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.4, Modifying Cash Services User Profiles.
Enter a one-time password, then enter it again to confirm. The password must be 8 to 20 characters and it must contain:
ГўВҲВ’
at least one capital letter,
ГўВҲВ’
one number, and
ГўВҲВ’
one of the following characters: @, #, $, %.
If the password format is incorrect, youГўВҖВҷll get an error message. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Error Message for Incorrect Password Format
Select the Temporary Password box to prompt the user to change the password at the next login. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Resetting Password
Select
Update User
to save the change. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Saving a Password Reset
Communicate the temporary password directly to the Cash Services User.
Note:	Users must update temporary passwords within 48 hours.
6.6	Changing a UserГўВҖВҷs Status to Inactive
Admin Users and Access Management Users can change the status of other usersГўВҖВҷ accounts to ГўВҖВңInactiveГўВҖВқ for long-term leaves.
To change a userГўВҖВҷs account to ГўВҖВңInactiveГўВҖВқ:
Log in to the application as described in Section 5.2, Accessing Cash Services via the Client Centre.
Select
Configuration
>
Profiles
>
User
. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Logging in to Modify User Profiles
Locate the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.4, Modifying Cash Services User Profiles.
Select ГўВҖВңInactiveГўВҖВқ from the Status drop-down list, then select
Update User
. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: Changing a UserГўВҖВҷs Status
When a userГўВҖВҷs status is ГўВҖВңInactiveГўВҖВқ, the user canГўВҖВҷt access Cash Services. An Admin User or Access Management User can edit the profile and change the status back to ГўВҖВңActiveГўВҖВқ to reinstate access when required.
6.7	Unlocking a UserГўВҖВҷs Account
The system locks an account if a user makes three invalid login attempts. The status of the locked account is automatically changed to ГўВҖВңInactiveГўВҖВқ.
To unlock an account, Admin Users or Access Management Users:
Log in to Cash Services as described in Section 5.2, Accessing Cash Services via the Client Centre.
Select
Configuration
>
Profiles
>
User
. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Logging in to Modify User Profiles
Select
Inactive
as the status type. The list of inactive users is displayed. Inactive users whose accounts are locked will have a lock icon displayed in the Actions column. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Searching for Inactive Users with Locked Status
Alternatively, you can do a direct search for the locked user:
ГўВ‘
Enter the userГўВҖВҷs login ID or name in the search field. See Figure 19.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select
All
(or
Inactive
) as the Status filter.
ГўВ‘Вў
Select
Search
.
Figure 19: Searching for a Locked User
The locked user is displayed with a lock icon in the Actions column. See Figure 20.
Select the lock icon to unlock the userГўВҖВҷs account. The account status automatically changes to active, and the user can now log in using their current password.
Note:	If you also need to reset the userГўВҖВҷs password, select the Edit icon in the Actions column, change the status to active, and reset the password. See Section 6.5, Resetting a UserГўВҖВҷs Password.
Communicate the temporary password directly to the Cash Services User, if applicable.
Note:	Users must update temporary passwords within 48 hours.
Figure 20: Unlocking an Account
6.8	Requesting an Account or Password Reset from Central 1
If an Admin User or Access Management User isnГўВҖВҷt available at your financial institution to reset a password or unlock an inactive account, you can send a request to Central 1.
To send a request to Central 1:
Complete the Cash Services Account or Password Reset Request, Form 9207. See Figure 21.
Have the form signed by two signing officers at your financial institution.
Send the signed form to Central 1 as directed on the form.
Central 1 will communicate the temporary password directly to the Cash Services User. The individual must update the password within 48 hours of receiving it.
Figure 21: Cash Services Account or Password Reset Request, Form 9207
Related Information
Cash Services Account or Password Reset Request,
Form 9207
6.9	Adding or Removing Access to Locations
Admin Users and Access Management Users can add and remove access to locations for other Cash Services Users, but only for the locations they have access to.
To add or remove access to locations in Cash Services:
Log in to Cash Services as described in Section 5.2, Accessing Cash Services via the Client Centre.
Select
Configuration
>
Profiles
>
User
. See Figure 22.
Figure 22: Logging in to Modify User Profiles
Locate the user profile as described in Section 6.4, Modifying Cash Services User Profiles.
Add or remove locations as follows. See Figure 23.
To add a location:
ГўВ‘
Enter the location number if youГўВҖВҷre searching for a specific location.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Alternatively, select applicable locations from the Available Locations column and select
Add
>>
to assign the location.
To remove a location:
ГўВ‘Вў
Select a location from the Assigned Locations column and select
Remove <<
to remove the location.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Select
Update User
to save the changes to the userГўВҖВҷs profile.
Figure 23: Adding or Removing Location Access in Cash Services
6.10	Cancelling a UserГўВҖВҷs Account in Cash Services
Admin Users and Access Management Users can cancel an inactive userГўВҖВҷs account when the profile is no longer needed in Cash Services. A user must first be marked as inactive before their status can be changed to ГўВҖВңNo Longer UsedГўВҖВқ. Once a user profile is marked as ГўВҖВңNo Longer UsedГўВҖВқ it canГўВҖВҷt be reactivated. A new profile would have to be created instead.
To change an inactive userГўВҖВҷs account to ГўВҖВңNo Longer UsedГўВҖВқ:
Log in to the application as described in Section 5.2, Accessing Cash Services via the Client Centre.
Select
Configuration
>
Profiles
>
User
. See Figure 24.
Figure 24: Configuring a User Profile
Select
Inactive
from the Status drop-down menu, then select the edit icon in the Actions column beside the applicable user. See Figure 25.
Figure 25: Selecting an Inactive User
Use the Status drop-down menu to select ГўВҖВңNo Longer UsedГўВҖВқ, then select
Update User
. See Figure 26.
Figure 26: Marking a Profile as No Longer Used
6.11	Removing a UserГўВҖВҷs Access to Cash Services
If a Cash Services User is a Client Centre User who no longer requires access to Cash Services, have a Security Officer remove access to the Cash Services application from the individualГўВҖВҷs Client Centre User profile in User Management or CPS Admin, as applicable.
If the individual who no longer requires access to Cash Services is a Security Officer, complete the CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035, and submit it as directed on the form. See the User Management Part or CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on modifying or deleting Client Centre Users.
Related Information
CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request,
Form 2035
END
